task_completion.description: Suzanne (Monkey) mesh is added at (0, 0, 1.5) and the BaseCube is not obstructing the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows a Suzanne monkey head positioned above the grid floor. The default cube is either deleted, hidden, or moved away so it does not block the monkey head.

task_completion.description: A Hair particle system is applied to the Suzanne mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Hair strands are visible emitting from the Suzanne mesh in the 3D viewport. The Particle Properties tab is active and shows a 'Hair' system selected instead of 'Emitter'.

task_completion.description: Emission particle count is at least 5,000 and hair length is approximately 0.15.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Particle Properties tab under the 'Emission' panel, the 'Number' field is set to 5000 or higher, and the 'Hair Length' field is set between 0.05 and 0.30.

task_completion.description: Simple children are enabled with a render amount of at least 50.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Particle Properties tab under the 'Children' panel, the 'Simple' button is selected, and the 'Render Amount' field is set to 50 or higher.

task_completion.description: A warm brown/orange material using a Principled BSDF is created and assigned to Suzanne.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Material Properties tab shows a Principled BSDF shader with the Base Color set to a warm brown/orange. If the color picker is visible, Red > Green > Blue, Red is >= 0.4, and Blue is <= 0.2. Suzanne appears brown/orange in the material preview or rendered viewport.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as fur_setup.blend and the rendered image is saved as fur_render.png in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title shows 'fur_setup.blend'. A file browser, terminal, or Blender's save dialog shows 'fur_render.png' and 'fur_setup.blend' located in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to perform the tasks rather than executing a pre-written Python script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating Blender menus, clicking property panels, and interacting with the 3D viewport.

integrity.description: The rendered image is a genuine output from Blender matching the scene setup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender render window (F12) is visible showing the monkey with fur, or the saved image exactly matches the geometry, material, and particle setup seen in the viewport.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Blender application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The full Blender interface is visible in the screenshots, not just a text editor or terminal running background commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected blend file path: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/fur_setup.blend. Expected render file path: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/fur_render.png. Minimum hair count: 5000. Minimum children render amount: 50. Hair length must be between 0.05 and 0.30. Target color constraints for the material: Warm Brown/Orange where R > G > B, R >= 0.4, and B <= 0.2. Minimum render size: 50 KB.
